input.title: Product rule applies when a counting problem

text_blocks.text: can be broken into multiple tasks

runs.text: can be broken into multiple tasks

text_blocks.text: The Product Rule: Suppose a procedure can be broken

runs.text: Suppose a procedure can be broken

text_blocks.text: into a sequence t1, t2, …, tk of tasks. Further, let there
be n1, n2, …, nk ways to complete each task. Then there

runs.text: ways to complete each task. Then there

text_blocks.text: are n1×n2×…×nk ways to complete the procedure.
To apply the product rule, do the following:

runs.text: To apply the product rule, do the following:

text_blocks.text: Identify each task t1, …, tk
For each task ti, determine the ni, the number of possible
ways to complete ti
Compute n1×n2×…×nk
Let’s look at a few examples…

runs.text: Let’s look at a few examples…

text_blocks.text: An example: Assigning offices
Example: It is Sherif and Bill’s first day of work at Pitt.

runs.text: It is Sherif and Bill’s first day of work at Pitt.

text_blocks.text: If there are 10 unused offices in their department, how

runs.text: If there are 10 unused offices in their department, how

text_blocks.text: many ways can Sherif and Bill be assigned an office?

runs.text: many ways can Sherif and Bill be assigned an office?

text_blocks.text: Step 1: Determine tasks
Give Sherif an office
Give Bill an office
Step 2: Count possible completions
Can give any one of 10 offices to Sherif

runs.text: Can give any one of 10 offices to Sherif

text_blocks.text: Can give any one of the remaining 9 offices to Bill

runs.text: Can give any one of the remaining 9 offices to Bill

l
Sherif and Bill can be assigned offices in 10×9 = 90 ways!

Example: How many bit strings of length 5 are there?

text_blocks.text: Solution:

l Task 2: Choose second bit
l Task 3: Choose third bit
l Task 4: Choose fourth bit
l Task 5: Choose fifth bit
l So, there are 2×2×2×2×2 = 25= 32 bit strings of length 5
